title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: ksh bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for ksh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: KornShell (ksh) is a Unix shell developed by AT&T Bell Laboratories, which is backward-compatible with the Bourne shell (sh) and includes many features of the C shell. The most recent version is KSH-93. KornShell complies with the POSIX.2 standard (IEEE Std 1003.2-1992).Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s):  stty -echo in scripts no longer works (JIRA:RHEL-112564)

Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian

Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian - Extended Update Support

full_text:
An update for ksh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

KornShell (ksh) is a Unix shell developed by AT&T Bell Laboratories, which is backward-compatible with the Bourne shell (sh) and includes many features of the C shell. The most recent version is KSH-93. KornShell complies with the POSIX.2 standard (IEEE Std 1003.2-1992).Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s):  stty -echo in scripts no longer works (JIRA:RHEL-112564)
